name: get_message_events
description: Search individual message events to track what happened to your emails. Events include delivery, bounce, click, open, spam_complaint, injection, policy_rejection, and more. Filter by recipient, sending domain, campaign, template, and date range. Data is retained for 10 days. For longer-term aggregate data, use the deliverability metrics endpoint instead.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start datetime in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:ssZ). Defaults to 24 hours ago.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End datetime in ISO 8601 format. Defaults to 1 minute ago.

parameters.name: events
parameters.description: Comma-delimited list of event types to filter. Options: delivery, bounce, click, open, spam_complaint, injection, policy_rejection, delay, generation_failure, generation_rejection, list_unsubscribe, link_unsubscribe.

parameters.name: recipients
parameters.description: Comma-delimited list of recipient email addresses to filter.

parameters.name: sending_domains
parameters.description: Comma-delimited list of sending domains to filter.

parameters.name: campaigns
parameters.description: Comma-delimited list of campaign IDs to filter.

parameters.name: templates
parameters.description: Comma-delimited list of template IDs to filter.

parameters.name: transmissions
parameters.description: Comma-delimited list of transmission IDs to filter.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results per page. Range: 1-10,000. Default: 1,000.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor. Use 'initial' for the first page, then use the cursor from the response for subsequent pages.
